title: Tapping to See Wealth as Endless
subtitle: A tapping practice for shifting from scarcity thinking to a sense of limitless supply
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 57 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the belief that wealth is a fixed pie with a limited share available — that more for one person means less for another. The reframe moves toward a felt sense of abundance as a continuous, expanding current.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 57 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you catch yourself thinking that someone else's financial success reduces what is available to you, or when abundance feels like luck rather than something that can be constant.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 57 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the scarcity-pie belief: the idea that wealth is finite and that every gain someone else makes is ground you can't reach. The reframe moves toward wealth as an expanding current — one that does not run out when it flows.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I used to think money is limited, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worried there's only so much to go around, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've felt like wealth might run out, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I once believed money is a fixed pie.
Side of eye: I thought more for me meant less for others.
Under eye: I feared the world could dry up.
Under nose: I braced for the end of good fortune.
Chin: I tightened my spending to stay safe.
Collarbone: I limited what I expected to receive.
Under arm: I'm ready to see abundance differently.
Top of head: I choose to believe wealth is infinite.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: There is more than enough for everyone.
Side of eye: Money keeps circulating and growing.
Under eye: I let abundance expand without limits.
Under nose: I expect resources to keep multiplying.
Chin: I feel safe in a world of endless supply.
Collarbone: I welcome constant new inflow.
Under arm: I enjoy wealth that does not run dry.
Top of head: I live in a world of limitless abundance.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 57 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
